name: DNS Resolution Failure
description: Diagnose and resolve DNS resolution failures affecting AD operations
author: AD Identity Control Plane

steps.title: Test DNS Resolution from Affected DC
steps.description: Verify whether the DC can resolve its own name and the domain name.

steps.title: Check SRV Records
steps.description: DNS A records work but SRV records may be missing. Check _ldap._tcp records.

steps.title: DNS Service Status
steps.description: Is the DNS Server service running on this DC?

options.label: DC is not a DNS server

steps.title: Stale DNS Record
steps.description: DNS resolves to a wrong IP address. This indicates a stale record. Check: 1) Scavenging settings on the DNS zone, 2) Static vs dynamic registration, 3) Whether the old IP belongs to a decommissioned DC. Clean up the stale record and allow the DC to re-register.

steps.title: SRV Record Status
steps.description: Are the SRV records present and pointing to the correct DCs?

steps.title: Check DNS Zones
steps.description: DNS service is running but resolution fails. Check: 1) Are forward lookup zones loaded? 2) Is the _msdcs zone present? 3) Are zone transfers working? 4) Check DNS event log for errors. Run: dnscmd /enumzones

steps.title: Restart DNS Service
steps.description: The DNS service is stopped. Before restarting, check the DNS event log for why it stopped. Common causes: corrupted zone file, insufficient permissions, port conflict. After checking, restart the service and verify zones load correctly.

steps.title: Check DNS Client Configuration
steps.description: This DC is not running DNS. Verify its DNS client settings point to working DNS servers: 1) Check preferred/alternate DNS server IPs, 2) Ensure those DNS servers are reachable, 3) The DC should preferably point to itself or another DC in the same site.

steps.title: Re-register DNS Records
steps.description: Force the DC to re-register its DNS records. Run 'nltest /dsregdns' on the affected DC (requires console access). Then restart the Netlogon service. Wait 5 minutes and verify records are created.

steps.title: Client-Side DNS Issue
steps.description: DNS records are correct on the server side. The issue may be client-specific: 1) Flush DNS cache: ipconfig /flushdns, 2) Check client DNS settings, 3) Test from multiple clients, 4) Check for DNS proxy or firewall interference.

steps.title: Verify DNS Resolution
steps.description: Confirm DNS resolution is working after remediation.

steps.title: Document Resolution
steps.description: Record: 1) Which DNS records were affected, 2) Root cause (service, zone, registration, stale record), 3) Remediation steps taken, 4) Verify AD-dependent services are working (LDAP, Kerberos, replication).
